ayahs.textUthmaniTajweed: عَبَسَ وَتَوَلَّىٰٓ ١
ayahs.translation: He [i.e., the Prophet (ﷺ) ] frowned and turned away

ayahs.textUthmaniTajweed: أَن جَآءَهُ ٱلْأَعْمَىٰ ٢
ayahs.translation: Because there came to him the blind man, [interrupting].

ayahs.textUthmaniTajweed: وَمَا يُدْرِيكَ لَعَلَّهُۥ يَزَّكَّىٰٓ ٣
ayahs.translation: But what would make you perceive, [O Muḥammad], that perhaps he might be purified

ayahs.textUthmaniTajweed: أَوْ يَذَّكَّرُ فَتَنفَعَهُ ٱلذِّكْرَىٰٓ ٤
ayahs.translation: Or be reminded and the remembrance would benefit him?

ayahs.textUthmaniTajweed: أَمَّا مَنِ ٱسْتَغْنَىٰ ٥
ayahs.translation: As for he who thinks himself without need,

ayahs.textUthmaniTajweed: فَأَنتَ لَهُۥ تَصَدَّىٰ ٦
ayahs.translation: To him you give attention.

ayahs.textUthmaniTajweed: وَمَا عَلَيْكَ أَلَّا يَزَّكَّىٰ ٧
ayahs.translation: And not upon you [is any blame] if he will not be purified.

ayahs.textUthmaniTajweed: وَأَمَّا مَن جَآءَكَ يَسْعَىٰ ٨
ayahs.translation: But as for he who came to you striving [for knowledge]

ayahs.textUthmaniTajweed: وَهُوَ يَخْشَىٰ ٩
ayahs.translation: While he fears [Allāh],

ayahs.textUthmaniTajweed: فَأَنتَ عَنْهُ تَلَهَّىٰ ١٠
ayahs.translation: From him you are distracted.

ayahs.textUthmaniTajweed: كَلَّآ إِنَّهَا تَذْكِرَةٌ ١١
ayahs.translation: No! Indeed, they [i.e., these verses] are a reminder;

ayahs.textUthmaniTajweed: فَمَن شَآءَ ذَكَرَهُۥ ١٢
ayahs.translation: So whoever wills may remember it.

ayahs.textUthmaniTajweed: فِى صُحُفٍ مُّكَرَّمَةٍ ١٣
ayahs.translation: [It is recorded] in honored sheets,

ayahs.textUthmaniTajweed: مَّرْفُوعَةٍ مُّطَهَّرَةِۭ ١٤
ayahs.translation: Exalted and purified,

ayahs.textUthmaniTajweed: بِأَيْدِى سَفَرَةٍ ١٥
ayahs.translation: [Carried] by the hands of messenger-angels,

ayahs.textUthmaniTajweed: كِرَامِۭ بَرَرَةٍ ١٦
ayahs.translation: Noble and dutiful.

ayahs.textUthmaniTajweed: قُتِلَ ٱلْإِنسَـٰنُ مَآ أَكْفَرَهُۥ ١٧
ayahs.translation: Destroyed [i.e., cursed] is man; how disbelieving is he.

ayahs.textUthmaniTajweed: مِنْ أَىِّ شَىْءٍ خَلَقَهُۥ ١٨
ayahs.translation: From what thing [i.e., substance] did He create him?

ayahs.textUthmaniTajweed: مِن نُّطْفَةٍ خَلَقَهُۥ فَقَدَّرَهُۥ ١٩
ayahs.translation: From a sperm-drop He created him and destined for him;

ayahs.textUthmaniTajweed: ثُمَّ ٱلسَّبِيلَ يَسَّرَهُۥ ٢٠
ayahs.translation: Then He eased the way for him;

ayahs.textUthmaniTajweed: ثُمَّ أَمَاتَهُۥ فَأَقْبَرَهُۥ ٢١

words.translation: and provides a grave for him

ayahs.translation: Then He causes his death and provides a grave for him.

ayahs.textUthmaniTajweed: ثُمَّ إِذَا شَآءَ أَنشَرَهُۥ ٢٢
ayahs.translation: Then when He wills, He will resurrect him.

ayahs.textUthmaniTajweed: كَلَّا لَمَّا يَقْضِ مَآ أَمَرَهُۥ ٢٣
ayahs.translation: No! He [i.e., man] has not yet accomplished what He commanded him.

ayahs.textUthmaniTajweed: فَلْيَنظُرِ ٱلْإِنسَـٰنُ إِلَىٰ طَعَامِهِۦٓ ٢٤
ayahs.translation: Then let mankind look at his food -

ayahs.textUthmaniTajweed: أَنَّا صَبَبْنَا ٱلْمَآءَ صَبًّا ٢٥
ayahs.translation: How We poured down water in torrents,

ayahs.textUthmaniTajweed: ثُمَّ شَقَقْنَا ٱلْأَرْضَ شَقًّا ٢٦
ayahs.translation: Then We broke open the earth, splitting [it with sprouts],

ayahs.textUthmaniTajweed: فَأَنۢبَتْنَا فِيهَا حَبًّا ٢٧
ayahs.translation: And caused to grow within it grain

ayahs.textUthmaniTajweed: وَعِنَبًا وَقَضْبًا ٢٨
ayahs.translation: And grapes and herbage

ayahs.textUthmaniTajweed: وَزَيْتُونًا وَنَخْلاً ٢٩
ayahs.translation: And olive and palm trees

ayahs.textUthmaniTajweed: وَحَدَآئِقَ غُلْبًا ٣٠
ayahs.translation: And gardens of dense shrubbery

ayahs.textUthmaniTajweed: وَفَـٰكِهَةً وَأَبًّا ٣١
ayahs.translation: And fruit and grass -

ayahs.textUthmaniTajweed: مَّتَـٰعًا لَّكُمْ وَلِأَنْعَـٰمِكُمْ ٣٢
ayahs.translation: [As] enjoyment [i.e., provision] for you and your grazing livestock.

ayahs.textUthmaniTajweed: فَإِذَا جَآءَتِ ٱلصَّآخَّةُ ٣٣
ayahs.translation: But when there comes the Deafening Blast

ayahs.textUthmaniTajweed: يَوْمَ يَفِرُّ ٱلْمَرْءُ مِنْ أَخِيهِ ٣٤
ayahs.translation: On the Day a man will flee from his brother

ayahs.textUthmaniTajweed: وَأُمِّهِۦ وَأَبِيهِ ٣٥
ayahs.translation: And his mother and his father

ayahs.textUthmaniTajweed: وَصَـٰحِبَتِهِۦ وَبَنِيهِ ٣٦
ayahs.translation: And his wife and his children,

ayahs.textUthmani: لِكُلِّ ٱمْرِئٍ مِّنْهُمْ يَوْمَئِذٍ شَأْنٌ يُغْنِيهِ
ayahs.textUthmaniTajweed: لِكُلِّ ٱمْرِىٍٕ مِّنْهُمْ يَوْمَئِذٍ شَأْنٌ يُغْنِيهِ ٣٧
ayahs.textIndopak: لِكُلِّ امۡرِیءٍ مِّنۡهُمۡ يَوۡمَٮِٕذٍ شَاۡنٌ يُّغۡنِيۡهِؕ‏
ayahs.translation: For every man, that Day, will be a matter adequate for him.
ayahs.transliteration: Likul limri-im minhum yawmaa-izin shaa-nuy yughneeh

ayahs.textUthmaniTajweed: وُجُوهٌ يَوْمَئِذٍ مُّسْفِرَةٌ ٣٨
ayahs.translation: [Some] faces, that Day, will be bright -

ayahs.textUthmaniTajweed: ضَاحِكَةٌ مُّسْتَبْشِرَةٌ ٣٩
ayahs.translation: Laughing, rejoicing at good news.

ayahs.textUthmaniTajweed: وَوُجُوهٌ يَوْمَئِذٍ عَلَيْهَا غَبَرَةٌ ٤٠
ayahs.translation: And [other] faces, that Day, will have upon them dust.

ayahs.textUthmaniTajweed: تَرْهَقُهَا قَتَرَةٌ ٤١
ayahs.translation: Blackness will cover them.

ayahs.textUthmaniTajweed: أُوْلَـٰٓئِكَ هُمُ ٱلْكَفَرَةُ ٱلْفَجَرَةُ ٤٢
ayahs.translation: Those are the disbelievers, the wicked ones.
